flights you columnist in magazine- decide to write 15-line article in which you try to highlight the inconveniences and risks of space tourism and to draw the readers attention to more important human issues -3- the text 1 the traditional school exercise book and pen could become items mentioned only in history lessons every pupil at wrightington school is being issued with their own laptop to use in class and for homework in what is understood to be first for british state schools all 1,400 boys and girls will have dell net book worth around 400 these will allow pupils at the school near radstock in somerset to hand in work online and receive instant feedback from teachers when they are not in the classroom 2 the machines which can be used for presentations and research are being issued part of 25 million project to create purpose built business and enterprise college buildings housing the internet servers and wireless systems will open after the easter break parents are being asked to contribute 2 per week to hire their child s laptop for the duration of their time at the school 3 deputy headmaster will roberts said parents think it is bargain because laptops are expensive to buy outright we are business and enterprise college and this is how it is in the business world so why not start they mean to go he added any time they want to learn they can they can work wherever they are in the school at home their work can be marked instantly and teacher scan offer feedback and share pupils work away from the classroom 4 the school bought the net books at discount with an estimated outlay of more than400,000 pupils who are aged between 11 and 18 can complete homework by connecting to the school servers from home and view information from the day s lessons their laptops are backed up the school s main hard drives every time they connect to the network roberts insisted however that children will continue to use traditional learning materials there will always be place for traditional writing and textbooks here he said but we want to prepare our pupils for the real world 5 ninety per of parents agreed to pay the 2-a week fee which contributes towards purchase and insurance costs those unable to will receive financial assistance the project was launched with help from government backed charity e- learning foundation which ensures it access in schools spokesman kevin pay said using computers boosts pupils work rates after all there is no excuse for not having homework you adapted news week 2006

very touristy but love the mountains and the purity of air -2- but at university and in my twenties went to most countries in europe well to india and the usa i d go somewhere each time sometimes my of course parts of america are like that too cape town is place of extremes of the very rich and the very poor but the scenery is insely awe inspiring -3- mostly i d splash out somewhere really nice west are true the sunday times august 22 2004 adapted

text: [SUBJECT]anglais [TYPE]principal [LEVEL]bac khaled ibn el walid end of term english test n 3 s dziri secondary school may 2014 4th form arts the reading passage 1 day after day he job applications and day after day the envelopes that returned contained rejections worse still bills he is just one of thousands of africans who leave the continent each year in the hope of starting new life abroad knew it was going to be difficult and was going to have to do menial jobs had been warned but there was no way anyone could really prepare he recalls abdul 30 arrived in london three years ago from ghana after emigrating because his girlfriend moved to the uk back in ghana his career progression had been seamless 2 first class degree in arts and graphics led to job at one of the country s most respected advertising agencies where he spent three years handling major accounts for high ile companies after moving to norbury in south london abdul completed one year diploma in video production at lambeth college while working part time at starbucks but the search for full time work after the course ended proved to be difficult and abdul took jobs at mcdonalds and ikea to make ends meet it was difficult to get job that matched my experience and was adamant that wanted creative job like the one had in ghana he says got dozens of rejection letters mostly was told that my capital the alarming exodus of america s best and brightest david heenan explains in the thesis that although for centuries many of the world s most talented scientists and mathematicians regarded the united states the promised land now the tide has turned america s foreign born superstars many of our best and brightest in science and technology are deciding that there s no place like home and they are heading back to their home countries at the alarming rate of up to 1,000 per day for more than five years heenan crisscrossed the globe traveling to eight countries three continents ireland iceland india china taiwan singapore and mexico to study what he calls america s reverse brain drain if the exodus continues heenan warns america s technological and scientific prominence and its economic strength will be in jeopardy for example more than 5,000 highly qualified people have repatriated to india from the u.s. in the last two years alone what s luring them back for the most part it s huge potential market in one of the world s fastest growing economies coupled with vastly improved living standards ireland is mission to bring its brainpower back home with massive development plan costing some 2 billion according to one of its initiatives enterprise ireland more than 75 of its targets some of america s most distinguished life scientists and researchers report that they want to return home within five years so america has two choices either cultivate its homegrown talent staunch the exodus of repatriates heenan urges idiate action both options offering various several months with the pyramids found that actually seeing the tajmahal is so astonishing it makes you catch your breath we did long overnight train journeys to calcutta and orissa and what s so fantastic about those train journeys is how you can see the whole indian life style from the train people getting up in the morning washing feeding their children conducting their entire lives it s complete sightseeing experience in itself -2- but at university and in my twenties went to most countries in europe well to india and the usa i d go somewhere each time sometimes my own sometimes with friend and wanted to learn much about place reading about it and doing lot of sightseeing so i d really get sense of people and the culture -3- mostly i d splash out really nice holidays for few nights egypt was particularly thrilling because i d seen so many pictures of the pyramids atschool almost couldn t believe was really seeing them we also stayed by the red sea and then took trips to the desert we even spent night camping in the moonscape -6- just before brick lane was published wanted to go back to bangladesh with my husband to see where was born but couldn t get visa it was big when urban exodus visited mimi and richard s made in ghent farm farm store bakery and gathering place the apples were ripe for the picking and the fall harvest had started the farm store was bustling with people coming to buy meat and bread for their evening meals mimi was busy in the kitchen while richard was working various projects the farm now it is hard to believe that only four years ago this 75-acre farm was overgrown fields and few derelict buildings 2 mimi and richard started weekenders in columbia county they bought little farmhouse small plot of land and would come up to escape the madness of new york city the weekends with their two daughters each visit they found it harder and harder to return to new york richard had worked in the advertising world for many years and the constant travel and stress had left him ready for the next adventure 3 with their two daughters entering high school they thought long about what truly held meaning in their life they sold their home in the suburbs and moved into their little farmhouse richard commuted back and forth until they found their current farm just mile from their previous place in ghent new york they invested their life savings to build made in ghent knowing well that it meant their business had to become sustainable in order to not have to return to their previous city lives 4 made in ghent is constantly evolving and adding new offerings for their local and seasonal community in addition to running an animal welfare approved meat and egg business mimi makes bread both host farm dinners and run creative and business development workshops richard has channeled his former agency skills into his photography and has established himself s respected photojournalist in the hudson valley ten heart stopping minutes later the austrian landed back earth after reaching speeds of up to 1.166 km per hour and breaking three world records including becoming the world s first supersonic skydiver by breaking the sound barrier at mach 1.24.he was wearing specially designed survival that kept his body intact against the hugely varying pressures that marked his drop back to earth without it his blood would have boiled and his lungs might have exploded 2 baumgartner later told press conference when was standing there top of the world you become so humble you don t think about breaking records he admitted all he could think about was getting back alive but added sometimes you have to go up really high to see how small you are baumgartner was carried up into crystal clear skies by gigantic balloon that measured 30 million square cubic feet and whose skin was one tenth the thickness of sandwich bag at the bottom of the balloon was capsule in which baumgartner sat in his he reached the desired height baumgartner went through checklist of 40 items with his mentor joe kittinger the previous holder of the highest altitude manned balloon flight they decided to go ahead watched by record 8 million people the jump was streamed live youtube 3 the two and half hour journey upwards during which the curvature of the earth became visible and the skies gradually turned black was matched with rather more rapid descent three cameras attached to baumgartner s recorded his freefall of just over four minutes which failed to break the existing freefall record for duration and then the parachute opening the stunt which was seven years in the planning and sponsored by red bull drinks beat two of kittinger s records the retired us air force colonel previously held the 40 1 the traditional school exercise book and pen could become items mentioned only in history lessons every pupil at wrightington school is being issued with their own laptop to use in class and for not always reliable in other countries in fact remember situation when harrison was taking big spanish exam while we were in the jungle of costa rica he was sitting outside which is where he found the strongest wi fi signal there were monkeys swinging above his head he was taking the exam little over an hour into the exam he hit the submit button and lost his internet connection he was not happy camper but he went with the flow and contacted his teacher who reset the exam after several similar instances we decided to hold off taking any additional online s due to unreliable internet mike (4 marks) able in development inflicted many than consistently kids because children misbehave for a variety of different reasons. perhaps they dont understand the rules, they feel they need to assert their own autonomy, or maybe they wish to test the limits imposed on them. however, some children misbehave ........................they are experiencing internal distress: anger, frustration, disappointment, anxiety, or sorrow the younger a child is, the more likely he is to call attention to his distress through his behaviour. as a child matures, however, there is an expectation that he will be increasingly ........................to resolve much of his distress on his own and will express his feelings through words rather ........................outwardly directed misbehaviour. there are also children, however, whose behaviour is .........................troubling to others. ........................ these cases, the childrens behaviours are outside of the range of what is considered normal or acceptable for their level of........................perhaps most alarming is that ........................of these children show little remorse, guilt, or understanding of the damage and the ........................by their behaviour. 2 choose the right option. (4marks) it is essential to ingrain the importance of family into your children. they need to know that friends will come and go, but family is the one constant they will have in life. as a parent, it is your job to foster the bond (between-betwin-betwen) siblings, as well as among each parent and child. this is especially important in a single-(childs-children-child) family .if its feasible, expose (those-this-that) children to as ( much-many-more) time as possible with extended family, preferably cousins (which-who-what) are similar in age to them. there are many other ways (to help-help-helped) your child realize that family is of the utmost importance. this understanding will remain with them as they grow (in-off- up), marry, and embark on (parenthood themselves - parenthood themselfes-themselves parenthood). 3 provide the bracketed words with the right tense or form. text: [SUBJECT]anglais [TYPE]principal [LEVEL]2 s dziri end of semester 2 khaled ibn el walid english language test secondary school 3rd form may 2019 the text 1 marcolini 60 an italian businessman fell ill after using handset at work for up to hours every day for 12 years now italy s supreme court in rome has blamed his phone saying there is causal link between his illness and phone use the sun has reported marcolini said this is significant for many people wanted this problem to become public because many people still do not know the risks wanted it recognised that there was link between my illness and the use of mobile and cordless phones parents need to know their children are at risk of this illness 2 british scientists have claimed there is insufficient evidence to prove any link to it s huge potential market in one of the world s fastest growing economies coupled with vastly improved living standards ireland is mission to bring its brainpower back home with massive development plan costing some 2 billion according to one of its initiatives enterprise ireland more than 75 of its targets some of america s most distinguished life scientists and researchers report that they bill kaczor the associated press florida seventh grader kelsey anne hizer 13 was getting mostly s and f s and felt teachers at her ocala middle regular school weren t giving her the help she needed she was ready to give up but after switching from that very school kelsey anne is receiving more individual attention making a s and b s and enthusiastic about learning even though she has never been in the same room her teachers kelsey anne transferred to the orlando based florida virtual school one of the nation s oldest and largest online schools about 2,700 full time and 51,400 part time florida students in grades through 12 get lessons over the internet from teachers scattered across the state and nation they along with about 1,000 students around the world who pay tuition and an indeterminate number served through contracts with 35 other states communicate with their teachers and each other through chat rooms e-mail telephone and instant messaging it s more one one than regular school kelsey anne said it s more they re there they re listening virtual learning is becoming omnipresent at colleges and universities but remains in its infancy at the elementary and secondary level where skeptics have questioned such factors its cost and effect children s socialization virtual schools are growing fast though at an annual rate of about 25 percent there are 25 statewide state led programs and more than 170 virtual charter schools across the nation according to the north american council for online learning estimates of elementary and secondary students taking virtual s range from 500,000 to 1 million nationally compared to total public school enrollment of about 50 million florida virtual part of the state s public education system is the leader in innovation depth of courses rigor and enrollment said bill tucker chief operating officer at education sector washington d.c. think tank founded 10 years ago it offers more than 90 courses ranging from such basics english and math to specialties that include keyboarding computer programming web design chinese marine science earth space science macroeconomics and microeconomics for the answer of one question if you don t understand question move to the next write clear answers and do not write in more than the given space use quotation marks for the true false questions skim the text to answer the questions that is do not read the whole details read just to answer do not get frustrated when the text is long the longer it is the easier it will be do not spend more than 40 minutes for the reading comprehension 2 language read the whole paragraphs before starting the answers read the words before and after the blanks to know the right form in the matching activity match accident at work that left him with permanent injuries he s now volunteer with sporting chance project that engages older men in the north east in activity and exercise groups and runs regular bingo sessions the change that jeff has experienced in his life result of volunteering is something many of the 30,000 involved with our volunteering matters charity have also seen they do all kinds of volunteering in their community from befriending elderly and isolated people supporting families where children are at risk to giving someone lift to hospital appointment there is now broad agreement about the positive contribution volunteering can have for people s health and wellbeing volunteering helps reduce loneliness now recognised serious health risk and is one of the community led approaches that can help improve mental health volunteering can help to provide people with ways out of poverty by giving them new skills and confidence and aid social integration but while the benefits of volunteering are clear there is worrying evidence that the people who could benefit most from giving their time are precisely those least likely to be involved there are many reasons for people not to volunteer for older people the barriers can include poor health poverty lack of skills poor transport links having caring responsibilities such looking after grandchildren younger people can be deterred by feeling they don t have the time to volunteer not knowing anyone else who volunteers many also think volunteering is just for older people with time their hands theguardian.com text: [SUBJECT]anglais [TYPE]principal [LEVEL]bac teacher mestiri mourad jel secondary school 2010 2011 ministry of education end term english test n1 student s name: level 4th form info jel secondary school class timing 2 hour mark 30 i- reading 12 read the text and answer the questions below an e-learning project which will allow students teachers and guest speakers in locations to communicate virtual s was launched in edinburgh yesterday the scottish minister of education cathy jamieson and educationalists from across scotland attended the launch of flexible learning in the community which was initiated one and half years ago the launch celebrated the project which uses technology from polycom becoming fully operational videos were used to give virtual tour of secondary school to primary school pupils and then the primary school pupils had the opportunity to ask their secondary school counterparts about facilities at the school through video conferencing link the city is putting flexible learning to two other main uses software called kar2ouche which allows children to create quick time movies has been particularly useful in modern language teaching you might get some students who might be wee bit reluctant about speaking foreign language in front of the whole class but they are very happy to make movie dub foreign words and then show it to the class said iain stewart virtual learning environment provides web space for pupils and schools and allows pupils to download assignments at home and then upload completed work to their teacher s webspace ron carr
